data.content_ref: inline:The claim posits a coordinated revolutionary conspiracy across multiple polities via intermarriage and gift-exchange networks, but there is no contemporary textual or archaeological evidence of such transnational class consciousness or collective action among chariot-warrior aristocracies; the Hittite and Mycenaean records show no discourse of class struggle or coordinated rebellion, and the mechanism relies on an anachronistic Marxist framework that is not attested in the Late Bronze Age sources.
